title: Estimate latent tyre-state and degradation slope instead of using raw wear proxies only
summary: Recent F1 research models tyre degradation as a latent state with pit-stop resets and shows better predictive robustness than a simple AR baseline.
rationale: Recent F1 research models tyre degradation as a latent state with pit-stop resets and shows better predictive robustness than a simple AR baseline. That suggests your current explicit features like `TyreLife`, `Cumulative_Degradation`, and rolling lap deltas may be missing a hidden pace-loss state and its uncertainty.
implementation_hint: Within each driver-race-stint, residualize lap time against race/lap field pace, then fit a simple Kalman/state-space or even recursive linear trend model with reset at pit stops. Use filtered state, slope, acceleration, and state uncertainty as new features; also derive undercut pressure by comparing current latent degradation to fresh-tyre expectation.
expected_effect: Could add signal where raw tyre age is noisy across compounds, tracks, and weather, improving ranking inside the true pit window.
risk: A full Bayesian fit may be too slow; a simplified filter is safer but may underfit if race context is not removed first.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
